context.goal: Validate functional correctness of subtraction and overflow detection without relying on fixed latency.
stimulus_intent: Only deterministic, contract-supported stimulus is used; otherwise the case is downgraded.
Drive A and B with a mix of random, deterministic, and corner‑case values.
timing_assumptions: Use reset-safe or protocol-safe observation windows only.
Monitor outputs for an indeterminate number of cycles after stimulus; no fixed cycle count is assumed.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Exercise boundary‑value and width‑sensitive input patterns while preserving timing ambiguity.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['A', 'B']
Apply zero‑like, one‑like, all‑ones, and two's‑complement extreme patterns on A and B.
timing_assumptions: Use reset-safe or protocol-safe observation windows only.
Observe outputs within a flexible window after each stimulus; no fixed latency is assumed.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Verify that consecutive subtractions produce correct independent results without hidden state.
stimulus_intent: Apply two back‑to‑back subtraction stimuli without idle cycles.
timing_assumptions: Allow an indeterminate observation window after each stimulus; do not assume fixed latency.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Exercise signed overflow scenarios using two's‑complement extreme values.
stimulus_intent: Provide signed‑extreme operand pairs to trigger positive and negative overflow.
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs within a flexible window; no fixed cycle count is assumed.

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

generated_files.description: Marks the rendered cocotb test directory as a Python package.
Conservative DUT interface helpers derived from the contract artifact.
Thin environment wrapper that composes interface, timing-safe waits, and coverage helpers.
Structured oracle helper functions rendered from the oracle artifact.
Stable runtime helpers exposed to LLM-filled stimulus and oracle TODO blocks.
Coverage scaffold rendered from plan coverage tags for later execution stages.
Rendered basic cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Rendered edge cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Phase 4 executable Makefile shell; Phase 5 injects run-time variables and executes it.
